body.title: Fix: coherence inhibitor suppresses LLM replies (wrong metric for non-habits)
body.result: suppress_incoherent now gates on source_label.startswith('habit:'). LLM replies no longer suppressed. main.py caller passes _source_label. 15 tests. 49030c61.
body.description:
suppress_incoherent() gates on flagged=True which fires when Jaccard score < 0.10. Designed for habit misfires (cached template dumps). LLM cloud-interactive replies score 0.07-0.10 because conversational responses introduce new concepts rather than echoing prompt tokens — so suppress_incoherent() returns '' for them, silently dropping every non-habit web reply.

Fix: in suppress_incoherent(), only suppress when source_label starts with 'habit:'. LLM-sourced responses should be flagged/logged but never suppressed. Change signature to accept source_label; update caller at main.py:6735 to pass _source_label.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/response_coherence_inhibitor.py (suppress_incoherent signature + logic), wild_igor/igor/main.py (pass _source_label to suppress_incoherent)
**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — suppress_incoherent gate logic + caller update. OUT — changing Jaccard threshold, removing coherence check for habits, other inhibitors.
**Test plan:** (a) test that suppress_incoherent with source='habit:X' and flagged=True returns ''; (b) test that suppress_incoherent with source='llm_or_tier0' and flagged=True returns original response; (c) test that coherence drops for 01cb0591/dc50fdc5 style turns would no longer suppress. Verify the two dropped turns (score 0.070, 0.099, source=llm_or_tier0) would now pass through.
